bet: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guiberoua Bété.
boo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tiemacèwè Bozo.
book: noun. A collection of sheets of paper bound together to hinge at one edge, containing printed or written material, pictures, etc.
boot: noun. A heavy shoe that covers part of the leg.
bot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bongo.
bott: noun. Alternative spelling of bot (the larva of the botfly).
box: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Buamu.
keto: noun. The carbonyl group of a ketone.
kex: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kukna.
kob: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Kohoroxitari.
kobo: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Nigerian naira.
koto: noun. A Japanese stringed instrument having numerous strings, usually seven or thirteen, that are stretched over a convex wooden sounding
obe: noun. A particular subdivision of ancient Laconia.
oboe: noun. A soprano and melody wind instrument in the modern orchestra and wind ensemble.
oke: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Okpe (Southwestern Edo).
oxo: noun. A substituent oxygen atom connected to another atom by a double bond.
tet: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tetum.
text: noun. A writing consisting of multiple glyphs, characters, symbols or sentences.
textbook: noun. A coursebook, a formal manual of instruction in a specific subject, especially one for use in schools or colleges.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
too: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xicotepec de Juárez Totonac.
toot: noun. The noise of a horn or whistle.
tot: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Patla-Chicontla Totonac.
